title: Jewish Mentorship: How a Teacher-Student Relationship Works in Judaism

sources.content: Acquire for yourself a teacher and acquire for yourself a friend
I learned much from my teachers, more from my colleagues, and most from my students
As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another
Rabban Gamliel the son of Rabbi Judah the Prince used to say: Appoint for yourself a teacher
Moses and Joshua - the archetypal teacher-student relationship
Rav-Talmid relationship as sacred bond beyond academic instruction
Derech Eretz - proper conduct toward one's teacher

cultural_parallels: Filial piety extended to teachers (一日为师，终身为父)
key_concepts: Emunat Chachamim - faith in the sages
